compiled_state.title: As a post-tool-use hook user, all 5 post-* dispatchers fail open with JSONL error logging on unexpected crashes
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Upgrades all 5 post-* dispatchers from silent `trap 'exit 0' ERR` to the shared logging handler. Currently these dispatchers fail open silently — crashes are swallowed with no diagnostic information.

**Why**: Post-* dispatchers run after tool execution. A crash today produces no log entry, making it impossible to diagnose intermittent hook failures. Upgrading to the shared handler adds JSONL logging without changing the fail-open behavior.

**Scope**:
- IN: `dispatchers/post-bash.sh`, `dispatchers/post-edit.sh`, `dispatchers/post-write.sh`, `dispatchers/post-all.sh`, `dispatchers/post-agent.sh` — each replaces `trap 'exit 0' ERR` with the shared handler sourcing pattern and registration
- OUT: Hook lib functions (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all 5 post-* dispatcher files source `hook-error-handler.sh` with a fail-open guard and register `_dso_hook_err_handler` as their ERR trap, replacing the previous silent `trap 'exit 0' ERR`
  ← Satisfies: "All 5 post-* dispatchers have fail-open ERR traps using the shared handler"
- When this story is complete, an unexpected crash in any post-* dispatcher produces a JSONL log entry in `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl` and the dispatcher exits 0
  ← Satisfies: "All 5 post-* dispatchers have fail-open ERR traps using the shared handler"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all modified dispatcher error handling

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Same source guard pattern as S2: `source "$HOOKS_LIB_DIR/hook-error-handler.sh" 2>/dev/null || true`
- [Testing] Verify that the behavior change (silent exit 0 → logging exit 0) does not affect any test assertions that relied on the absence of log entries for post-* crashes

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body:
STATUS:complete
Tasks: T1=451f-2029 (RED tests for 5 post-* dispatchers JSONL logging), T2=36a8-27a9 (replace silent traps with logging handler). Dependency: T2 depends_on T1.
comments.author: Test
